disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recent heavy mosquito exposure — lives in a mosquito-prone outer-regional area and has spent time outdoors near water/wetlands in the past couple of weeks
trigger_question_examples: Have you been bitten by mosquitoes or spent time outdoors near water recently?
Any recent camping, gardening, or time in bushland or near wetlands?
Do you live somewhere with a lot of mosquitoes at the moment?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, actually — there've been heaps of mosquitoes where I live and I was out in the garden near the creek a fair bit last week. I've got a few bites.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Preceding maculopapular rash — a flat, blotchy red rash over the trunk and limbs about 3-4 days ago that has since faded
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me more about that rash — where was it and what did it look like?
Any rashes or skin changes before the joint pain started?
What did the rash look like and how long did it last?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It was a flat, blotchy red rash — bits of it on my trunk and arms, not really itchy. It came up maybe three or four days ago and it's pretty much gone now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Red-flag screen NEGATIVE — no single joint is bright red, hot to touch, or dramatically more swollen than the others; the pain is migratory and roughly symmetrical rather than fixed in one joint
trigger_question_examples: Is any one joint bright red, hot to touch, or much more swollen than the rest?
Is the pain stuck in a single joint, or does it move around between joints?
Does one particular joint feel hot and look angry red?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it kind of moves around and they all ache — none of them is bright red or hot to touch, they're just puffy and sore. It's not stuck in one spot.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: They're all just sore, I haven't really looked that closely.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Red-flag screen NEGATIVE — no high fever or shaking chills/rigors; only feels mildly warm, home thermometer 37.6 C
trigger_question_examples: Have you had a high fever, or shaking chills where you can't stop shivering?
What's the highest temperature you've measured, and any rigors?
Have you felt hot and cold with uncontrollable shivering?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel a bit warm but I haven't had proper shivering or a high temperature — my thermometer said 37.6 tonight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Significant functional impairment with social isolation — lives alone, unable to open bottles/jars or prepare food tonight, no one available to help
trigger_question_examples: How is this affecting you at home — is there anyone with you who can help?
Are you managing okay on your own with everyday tasks tonight?
Do you have someone who can check on you or help you eat and take medication?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm on my own and there's no one around tonight. I can't open my tablet bottles or make myself anything to eat because my wrists are so sore.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pharmacology screen — no known drug allergies; not on anticoagulants or regular anti-inflammatories; no history of stomach ulcers, kidney disease, or asthma (relevant to NSAID safety)
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any allergies, and are you on any regular medications or blood thinners?
Any history of stomach ulcers, kidney problems, or asthma?
Have you ever had a bad reaction to anti-inflammatory tablets like ibuprofen?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No allergies that I know of, and I don't take anything regular — no blood thinners. No stomach ulcers or kidney trouble, and I don't have asthma.
